title: Localized tailoring of three-dimensional articles via additive manufacturing
abstract: Additive manufacturing processes, systems and three-dimensional articles include the formation of voxels and/or portions of three-dimensional articles with different properties relative to other voxels and/or portions. The processes generally include changing one or more laser beam parameters including power level, exposure time, hatch spacing, point distance, velocity, and energy density during the formation of selected voxels and/or portions of the three-dimensional articles. Also disclosed are processes that include an additive manufacturing process that provides localized secondary heat treatment of certain voxels and/or regions at a temperature below the melting point of the three-dimensional article but high enough to effect a localized property change.
claims: 1. A process for modifying an existing three-dimensional article, comprising: additively manufacturing a three-dimensional structure by exposing a layer of powder to an energy source pattern, melting and/or sintering the powder to form a two dimensional structure in the layer, depositing an additional layer of the powder on the two dimensional structure and repeating exposing, melting and depositing to form the three-dimensional structure; and exposing the three-dimensional structure after additive manufacture to a secondary heat treatment comprising thermally exposing a selected voxel and/or portion of the three-dimensional article with an energy beam at a temperature less than a melting point of the existing three-dimensional article to effect a localized change in at least one property of the selected voxel and/or portion relative to other voxels and/or portions, wherein the voxel represents a volume element in a three-dimensional space within the three dimensional structure and the portion represents a cross section of the three-dimensional structure in its entirety.
